Purpose: Device management traffic — SSH access to all network devices and SNMP polling
Tagged Ports (Trunk): Core-SW-01 Gi0/1 (uplink to Access-SW-01), Access-SW-01 Gi0/1 (uplink to Core-SW-01)
Untagged Ports (Access): None — management traffic uses SVI only
Notes: Dedicated management VLAN isolates device administration traffic from user data. All SSH sessions and SNMP polling traverse this VLAN only. Never assign end-user hosts to this VLAN.

Tagged Ports (Trunk): Core-SW-01 Gi0/1 (uplink to Access-SW-01), Access-SW-01 Gi0/1 (uplink to Core-SW-01)
Untagged Ports (Access): Access-SW-01 access ports facing end hosts
Notes: Primary data VLAN for all end-user devices including MGMT-HOST-01. Inter-VLAN routing handled by Core-SW-01 Layer 3 SVIs.

Purpose: VoIP phone traffic — reserved for future IP telephony integration
Tagged Ports (Trunk): Core-SW-01 Gi0/1 (uplink to Access-SW-01), Access-SW-01 Gi0/1 (uplink to Core-SW-01)
Untagged Ports (Access): Access-SW-01 ports configured as voice VLAN on dual-purpose data/voice ports
Notes: VLAN pre-configured and trunked in anticipation of future VoIP device addition. Cisco best practice — voice and data traffic separated to enable QoS prioritization. No devices assigned yet.

Purpose: Network monitoring and telemetry traffic — SNMP traps and syslog
Tagged Ports (Trunk): Core-SW-01 Gi0/1 (uplink to Access-SW-01), Access-SW-01 Gi0/1 (uplink to Core-SW-01)
Notes: Isolates SNMP polling, syslog, and Grafana/Zabbix monitoring traffic from user data. Prevents monitoring overhead from impacting production VLAN performance.

Purpose: Hardened native VLAN — carries untagged trunk frames
Tagged Ports (Trunk): All trunk links between Core-SW-01 and Access-SW-01
Notes: CRITICAL SECURITY DECISION: Native VLAN changed from default VLAN 1 to VLAN 99 on all 802.1Q trunk links. VLAN 1 as native is a known attack vector (VLAN hopping). No devices are assigned to VLAN 99 — it exists solely as a hardened native VLAN. This is Cisco best practice and a deliberate security design choice.

Untagged Ports (Access): None — all ports removed from VLAN 1
Notes: VLAN 1 exists by default on all Cisco switches and cannot be deleted. All ports have been explicitly moved off VLAN 1. No management interfaces, no host ports, and no trunk native VLAN assignment use VLAN 1. Documented here to confirm intentional exclusion — not an oversight.
